_meta.purpose: Stage-0/1 per-profile cost estimate used by the SS5.1 admission rule before the SS5.3 duration-bucket predictor is validated and flagged on. One column (starting tier), not the full bucket x profile matrix.
_meta.status: UNPOPULATED. Every 'p50'/'p90'/'p95' below is null because no run-log telemetry exists yet. Do NOT invent numbers here -- design SS5.1/SS10.3 is explicit that ceilings must be runtime-calibrated, never hard-coded. Populate via populate_estimates.py once real tasks have run.
_meta.units: total tokens per task attempt, summed across the implementer + full validator panel for that profile
bootstrap_procedure: 1. Run at least MIN_SAMPLES_PER_PROFILE real tasks per profile through the loop (manually is fine for the first batch).
2. Append one JSONL line per completed task to a run-log: {"profile": "...", "total_tokens": N}.

3. Run: python3 populate_estimates.py <run-log.jsonl> --write. This recomputes p50/p90/p95 per profile from the log and rewrites this file's cost_estimate_by_profile block only -- risk_profiles and overshoot_variance_by_tier_effort are edited by hand.

4. Re-run periodically as more tasks complete; sample_size grows and estimates sharpen. Below MIN_SAMPLES_PER_PROFILE, the admission rule (SS5.1) should treat the estimate as low-confidence and forecast conservatively (e.g. widen the quantile used).

overshoot_variance_by_tier_effort._comment: Per (tier, effort) cell: how far actual spend tends to exceed the p50 estimate, since thinking budgets are soft targets with a long overshoot tail (design SS5.3, arXiv 2507.02076). Populate the same way once telemetry exists; until then the admission rule should assume a wide, conservative margin.
